{"sql":"SELECT table_name, column_name, data_type, is_nullable

INSERT creates one; UPDATE (WHERE id=…) changes state/assignee/priority.","volatility":"stable","ops":["read","insert!","update!"],"columns":[{"name":"id","type":"text","description":"e.g. ENG-123"},{"name":"title","type":"text"},{"name"…(+513)

text:
Perfect! Now I understand the structure:

1. `github_pull_requests` has:
   - `state` column with values: "open", "merged", "closed"
   - `closes_linear` column with Linear issue ID (if the PR closes one)
   
2. `linear_issues` has:
   - `s…(+218)

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"SELECT 
    pr.number as pr_number,
    pr.closes_linear as linear_issue_id,
    li.state as linear_issue_state
FROM github_pull_requests pr
INNER JOIN linear_issues li ON pr.closes_linear = li.id
WHERE pr.state = 'merged' 
    AND li.state != 'done'
ORDER BY pr.number"}

text: Now let me also get the total count

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"SELECT 
    COUNT(*) as total_count
FROM github_pull_requests pr
INNER JOIN linear_issues li ON pr.closes_linear = li.id
WHERE pr.state = 'merged' 
    AND li.state != 'done'"}
